input: Investigate Oligonychus sacchari resistance in IRC99-02 vs. CP69-1062 sugarcane varieties.
expected_output: IRC99-02 was the most susceptible variety to Oligonychus sacchari, while CP69-1062 was the most resistant variety.
context:
Field Evaluation of Sugarcane Varieties Against Yellow Mite Oligonychus sacchari McGregor (Prostigmata: Tetranychidae)
Amin Nikpay 1 • Francois-Regis Goebel 2
Received: 19 September 2017 / Accepted: 29 November 2017 Ó Society for Sugar Research & Promotion 2018
Abstract Sugarcane yellow mite, Oligonychus sacchari (Prostigmata: Tetranychidae), is one of the main detrimental pests in sugarcane growing areas in Iran. The early populations of mites colonize on the lower surfaces of sugarcane leaves. Because of high reproductive potential, multi-generation are produced per year under warm and humid temperature during sugarcane growing season. This pest imposes economic damage to sugar industries. Field trials were conducted on evaluation of varietal resistance of five sugarcane varieties against yellow mite during two successive seasons 2014-2015 and 2015-2016 in sugarcane fields at Salman Farsi Agro-industry unit in Ahwaz, Iran. After initiation of O. sacchari activity in fields, in each plot, fifteen leaves were selected at random and number of living mites was recorded. The results showed that there were significant differences among tested varieties. The variety IRC99-02 was the most susceptible in tested years by CP57-614 in the total number of living mites and dry-leaf even though CP69-1062 was the most resistant variety.
Keywords Sugarcane  Yellow mite  Multi-generation  Varieties  Population
Sugarcane is grown commercially in the subtropical regions of Iran. The most important region of sugarcane production in Iran is the Khuzestan province where it is

input: What were the specific mean living O. sacchari mite counts per leaf for each sugarcane variety on each 2016 sampling date?
How does O. sacchari resistance vary among the five sugarcane varieties?
expected_output: Variety IRC99-02 showed severe mite damage from O. sacchari. The context does not detail the resistance variation among the other four varieties (CP57-614, CP48-103, CP69-1062, and SP70-1143).
context:
Field Evaluation of Sugarcane Varieties Against Yellow Mite Oligonychus sacchari McGregor (Prostigmata: Tetranychidae)
Fig. 1 Severe mite damage on variety IRC99-02 (June 2016)
basic control strategies, varietal resistance is important way to manage sugarcane pests under a global scenario (Goebel and Sallam 2011). The aim of this study was to evaluate varietal resistance of five different commercial sugarcane varieties to O. sacchari under field conditions.
Trials were conducted in two successive years 2014-2015 and 2015-2016 at Salman Farsi Agro-industry Farms (48 ° 35 0 E, 31 8 8 0 S) Ahwaz, Iran. For this research study, five sugarcane varieties including IRC99-02 (cross made in Cuba and selected in Iran), CP57-614 (Canal Point USA), CP48-103 (Canal Point USA), CP69-1062 (Canal Point USA) and SP70-1143 (Sao Paulo Brazil) were used. These five selected varieties are cultivated extensively under Iranian sugarcane agro-industries.
The experiment used a randomized complete block design with four replicates. Each experimental plot (block) consisted of 8 rows 9 8 m in length and 1.8 m spaced (between two furrows) in different points of field (100.8 m 2 for each plot). This plot configuration was used for sugarcane experiments because plots for trials in sugarcane are recommended to be at least 25-m 2 (Laycock 2004). Each plot was separated by 10 m distance as buffer to build a protective barrier. Cultivation of each variety included standard tillage, complete plow-out of previous crop remaining following by ridging at 1.8-m furrow spacing. The whole field was fertilized according to local fertilizer recommendations to amend for any nutrient deficiencies. Following planting of seed cane sets (on each furrow); all furrows were treated with Atrazine and Sencor herbicides (2 ? 2 kg per hectare), as early post-emergence
application based on local recommendations for control of annual grass and broadleaf weeds.

input: Considering the 2015 field evaluation, which variety consistently had the lowest mean O. sacchari per leaf?
expected_output: CP69-1062 consistently had the lowest mean O. sacchari per leaf during the 2015 field evaluation.
